channel.title: Drupal Canvas
channel.description: Stay informed about developments in Drupal Canvas.

item.title: #3586613: Add content-entity-reference well-known prop shape for code components
item.description:
• Project: Drupal Canvas
  • Type: Major feature request
  • Version: 1.x-dev
  • Status: Fixed
  • Changes: 30 files, +1810/−76 · php +1703/−59 · md +59/−11 · yml +33/−6 · txt +5 · json +5 · twig +5
  • Diff: 29e4423
  • Discussion: #3586613 · 3 contributors · 24 comments
  • Followers: 4

Summary

Canvas code components can now declare a first-class content-entity-reference prop type. Instead of using a bare type: object and relying on implicit inference from dataDependencies.entityFields, component developers use $ref: json-schema-definitions://canvas.module/content-entity-reference to explicitly signal that a prop receives a content entity. At render time, Canvas resolves the referenced entity, evaluates all declared entity field expressions against it, and delivers a keyed payload to the component. The concrete entity type and bundle are derived at runtime from dataDependencies.entityFields (the single source of truth) and are never duplicated in the persisted prop schema.

Impact

Affects module maintainers.

  • New API: New prop shape json-schema-definitions://canvas.module/content-entity-reference; new public methods JavaScriptComponent::getContentEntityReferenceProps() and ::getEntityFieldExpressions(); new conditions property on SequenceKeysMustMatchConstraint; new BetterEntityDataDefinition::getEntityType() method.

Technical details

The new shape json-schema-definitions://canvas.module/content-entity-reference is added to schema.json and registered in canvas.json_schema.yml alongside image and video. The JsonSchemaObjectRef enum gains a ContentEntityReference case.

JavaScriptComponent::toSdcDefinition() now projects x-allowed-entity-type-id and, when the target entity type has bundles, x-allowed-bundle from dataDependencies.entityFields into the returned SDC definition array. The stored config entity is never mutated; the projection is local and idempotent. Two new public methods support this: ::getContentEntityReferenceProps() returns the subset of props that carry this shape (matched by normalized schema, not raw definition), and ::getEntityFieldExpressions() returns the declared field expression strings for a given prop.

At render time, JsComponent::getExplicitInput() iterates populated content-entity-reference props, reads the already-resolved EvaluationResult from the parent call, and evaluates each entity field expression against the resolved FieldableEntityInterface. Dangling references, empty fields, and unresolvable sources are silently skipped. Developer-facing payload keys are computed by JsComponent::generateKeyForExpression(): entity key names (e.g., label) take precedence over raw field names; reference traversal segments are joined with $ (valid in JS/TS identifiers but not in Drupal field machine names, preventing collisions).

ContentEntityReferenceObjectConstraint is registered as the JSON Schema object constraint in CanvasServiceProvider. It validates that x-allowed-entity-type-id resolves to a known content entity type and that x-allowed-bundle is present if and only if the entity type has bundles. The same logic is exposed as ::validateMetadataSchema() for use at metadata-derivation time by ComponentMetadataRequirementsChecker, which also enforces that content-entity-reference props are never required and never carry examples. The ad-hoc "entity-ref props cannot be required" check previously in JsComponentHasValidAndSupportedSdcMetadataConstraintValidator is removed, fully subsumed by this path.

SequenceKeysMustMatchConstraint gains a conditions property. In canvas.schema.yml, this is used to narrow the entityFields key-subset check so it only matches props that carry the content-entity-reference $ref, closing a @todo left by #3585298.

EntityFieldPropSourceMatcher::matchContentEntityReferenceShape() (private) matches host-entity reference fields to a content-entity-reference prop by comparing the field's referenceable entity type and bundle against x-allowed-entity-type-id / x-allowed-bundle from the projected schema.

JsonSchemaType::computeStorablePropShape() now handles this shape, returning a StorablePropShape with field type entity_reference, widget entity_reference_autocomplete, and target_type storage setting derived from x-allowed-entity-type-id; target_bundles is added to the instance settings when x-allowed-bundle is present.

Upgrade

Code component developers declare a content-entity-reference prop in their component's props definition:

article:
  title: Interesting article
  type: object
  $ref: json-schema-definitions://canvas.module/content-entity-reference

Rules that apply to this prop type:

  • The prop must not be listed in required; referenced entities may be deleted or absent.
  • Do not add x-allowed-entity-type-id or x-allowed-bundle to the persisted prop definition; JavaScriptComponent::toSdcDefinition() injects these from dataDependencies.entityFields at runtime.
  • Do not add examples; the entity value is resolved at runtime.

The prop receives a keyed object payload at render time. Keys follow entity key names where available (e.g., label) or field machine names otherwise. Reference traversal uses $ as a separator (e.g., image$url).

Callers integrating with JavaScriptComponent can now use:

  • JavaScriptComponent::getContentEntityReferenceProps(): array — returns the prop name-to-definition map for entity-reference props.
  • JavaScriptComponent::getEntityFieldExpressions(string $prop_name): list<string> — returns the entity field expression strings for a given entity-reference prop.

The new optional conditions property on SequenceKeysMustMatchConstraint filters which elements of the target sequence are considered when checking key membership:

SequenceKeysMustMatch:
  propertyPathToSequence: props
  conditions:
    type: object
    "$ref": "json-schema-definitions://canvas.module/content-entity-reference"

Contribution

Timeline

  • Opened: April 24, 2026
  • Committed: May 12, 2026 (18 days later)

Key contributors

  • penyaskito (Acquia)
  • longwave (Full Fat Things)
  • wim leers (Acquia)
  • effulgentsia (Acquia)
  • lauriii (Acquia)

Collaboration

wim leers marked the issue Needs Work after reviewing MR !992, identifying that the implementation blurred the separation of concerns between prop source matchers and suggesters that had been carefully established in a prior issue (#3555300). In response, penyaskito split the JsonSchemaObjectRef enum introduction into a prerequisite issue (#3587673) to keep the scope manageable. After further rounds of review with longwave, wim leers approved the final version, noting it was a "significant improvement" over his own original proof-of-concept MR (!622) and specifically highlighting the addition of JSON Schema validation that he had not written in his PoC.

This content is AI-generated and may contain errors. See Drupal Digests for more.

item.title: #3589128: Automate review using PHPStan: adopt shipmonk-rnd/dead-code-detector
item.description:
• Project: Drupal Canvas
  • Type: Major feature request
  • Version: 1.x-dev
  • Status: Fixed
  • Changes: 37 files, +1044/−232 · php +993/−230 · neon +47 · json +2/−1 · yml +1/−1 · txt +1
  • Diff: f9a8591
  • Discussion: #3589128 · 2 contributors · 14 comments
  • Followers: 3

Summary

The Canvas module has adopted shipmonk/dead-code-detector 1.1.1 as a PHPStan extension to automatically flag unused methods, properties, and classes during CI. Because Drupal's dynamic dispatch patterns produce many false positives, eleven custom usage providers were written to teach the detector about hooks, routing callbacks, DIC service calls, schema validation callbacks, and test framework conventions. Once false positives were eliminated, the analysis confirmed and removed approximately 200 lines of genuinely dead code: a whole exception class, several public methods, unused constructor parameters, and unreferenced test properties.

Impact

Affects module maintainers.

  • API change: Several public symbols removed as confirmed dead code: InvalidRequestBodyValue class, JsonSchemaType::isIterable() and ::isTraversable(), withDelta() on three prop expression classes, and three methods on CanvasResourceLinkCollection (which is @internal).

Technical details

The dead-code detector is wired into phpstan.neon by tagging eleven new provider classes with shipmonk.deadCode.memberUsageProvider. All providers live in phpstan_rules/Canvas/PHPStan/UsageProvider/ and extend ReflectionBasedMemberUsageProvider, returning VirtualUsageData for symbols Drupal invokes through paths static analysis cannot follow.

The providers cover: DrupalHookUsageProvider marks methods carrying #[Hook] and their DIC-instantiated constructors; DrupalRoutingUsageProvider parses all canvas *.routing.yml files at analysis time to identify _controller and _title_callback classes, and marks access() on classes under Drupal\canvas\Access\; DrupalServiceCallsUsageProvider parses *.services.yml calls: directives; ConfigSchemaValidationUsageProvider parses *.schema.yml callback: entries; DrupalTestUsageProvider handles LSB reads of test properties ($modules, $defaultTheme, $configSchemaCheckerExclusions, etc.), abstract-base property writes, and polymorphic test method overrides; CanvasApiContractUsageProvider suppresses false positives on all canvas interface and abstract-class methods and their implementations; CanvasTraitUsageProvider works around a PHP 8.0+ change where ReflectionMethod::getDeclaringClass() returns the using class instead of the trait, breaking ShipMonk's candidate matching; DrupalDynamicPropertyUsageProvider covers EntityBase and Constraint subclass properties written via $this->$key = $value dynamic assignment; DrupalConfigTranslationUsageProvider, DrupalPolymorphicDispatchUsageProvider, and ContribPluginsUsageProvider cover further dynamic dispatch patterns. Two submodules (canvas_ai, canvas_personalization) are excluded via ignoreErrors entries pending their own cleanup.

The confirmed dead code deletion removed: the InvalidRequestBodyValue exception class; JsonSchemaType::isIterable() and ::isTraversable(); withDelta() on FieldObjectPropsExpression, FieldPropExpression, and ReferenceFieldPropExpression; CanvasResourceLinkCollection::hasLinkWithKey(), filter(), and merge() (the collection class itself is @internal); CanvasPropVisitor::extractAttributeName(); ShapeMatchingHooks::getMediaSourceFieldName(); ConfigEntityVersionDeriver::$basePluginId; ComponentPluginManager::$isRecursing; injected dependencies removed from ValidComponentTreeItemConstraintValidator (TypedDataManagerInterface) and CanvasTemplateRenderer (ElementInfoManagerInterface, RequestStack, LanguageManagerInterface); and unused test class properties and imports. Symbols that must be kept but cannot yet be reached by analysis are suppressed inline with @phpstan-ignore shipmonk.deadMethod or @phpstan-ignore shipmonk.deadConstant.

Upgrade

If you reference any of the following removed symbols in custom code, remove the references:

// Deleted class — remove all usages:
\Drupal\canvas\InvalidRequestBodyValue

// Deleted enum methods:
JsonSchemaType::isIterable()
JsonSchemaType::isTraversable()

// Deleted methods on prop expression classes:
FieldObjectPropsExpression::withDelta(int $delta)
FieldPropExpression::withDelta(int $delta)
ReferenceFieldPropExpression::withDelta(int $delta)

// Deleted methods on CanvasResourceLinkCollection (@internal, but deletions are real):
CanvasResourceLinkCollection::hasLinkWithKey(string $key)
CanvasResourceLinkCollection::filter(callable $f)
CanvasResourceLinkCollection::merge($a, $b)

ValidComponentTreeItemConstraintValidator and CanvasTemplateRenderer no longer accept their previously injected services in their constructors; if you subclass or manually instantiate these, update the constructor calls accordingly.

Contribution

Timeline

  • Opened: May 8, 2026
  • Committed: May 12, 2026 (4 days later)

Key contributors

  • wim leers (Acquia)
  • penyaskito (Acquia)

Collaboration

wim leers drove the entire change. After getting stuck on the initial setup (the rules.neon include is optional, not required as the docs imply), he ran a series of CI iterations reducing false positives from 467 down to zero. He explicitly used Claude (claude-sonnet-4-6 and claude-haiku-4-5) to generate all eleven usage providers, spending $31.06 in API costs over roughly six wall-clock hours, and noted in the thread: "Everything outside [phpstan_rules/] was done manually" and "$31.06 + a few hours of my time to never have to chase dead code again seems well worth it."

This content is AI-generated and may contain errors. See Drupal Digests for more.

item.title: #3588661: Make CLI builds use shared Vite infrastructure
item.description:
• Project: Drupal Canvas
  • Type: Normal feature request
  • Version: 1.x-dev
  • Status: Fixed
  • Changes: 100 files, +2708/−1026 · js +1955/−879 · mdx +588/−145 · yml +114 · php +20/−2 · css +12 · json +10 · webp +4 · svg +3 · jpg +1 · mp4 +1
  • Diff: d0aec30
  • Discussion: #3588661 · 1 contributor · 6 comments
  • Followers: 3

Summary

The canvas build and canvas push commands now use a shared Vite-based build pipeline. This unlocks Vite-resolved import patterns that were not previously available in local Code Component builds: static asset imports (images, video, audio, fonts), SVG files imported as URLs, and SVG files imported as React components using the ?react suffix. Three additional packages (@drupal-api-client/json-api-client, drupal-jsonapi-params, and swr) are now listed as built-in bundled packages available to Code Components. A new canvas pull --include-brand-kit and canvas push --include-brand-kit flow lets you manage Brand Kit fonts from a local canvas.brand-kit.json file alongside Code Components. The documentation for Code Components has been substantially reorganized, with new pages covering local codebase setup, imports and assets, Brand Kit, and component metadata.

Impact

Affects module maintainers and site owners.

  • Config change: componentDir default changed from process.cwd() to src/components; must now be inside aliasBaseDir
  • New API: New buildCanvasProject() function in packages/cli/utils/build-project unifies CLI build and push pipelines

Technical details

The core change replaces the multi-step build pipeline in packages/cli with a single buildCanvasProject() call imported from utils/build-project. Previously, canvas build called analyzeAndBundleImports, buildComponent (per component), buildTailwindForComponents, and generateManifest as discrete steps. These are now unified under buildCanvasProject(), which returns a structured BuildResult carrying componentResults, tailwindResult, vendorImportCount, and localImportCount. Vite owns the browser graph, artifact output, vendor chunk splitting, and asset manifest entries.

canvas push now calls pushBuiltComponents instead of buildAndPushComponents. The push path no longer rebuilds components from source; it consumes the pre-assembled component payloads from the build result. Dependency ordering during upload is handled by inspecting importedJsComponents on each payload. Duplicate asset files referenced by multiple manifest entries are now uploaded once and the same URI is reused for each entry.

On the configuration side, componentDir defaults changed from process.cwd() to "src/components" for both the Workbench and the CLI canvas.config.json schema. The documentation also notes that componentDir must now be inside aliasBaseDir. Projects that relied on the old process.cwd() default or placed components outside aliasBaseDir need to add an explicit componentDir entry to canvas.config.json.

In CanvasBuilder.php, three entries were appended to the built-in packages list: json_api_client (@drupal-api-client/json-api-client), drupal_jsonapi_params, and swr. A pre-existing malformed code fence in the FormattedText example (missing opening backtick) was also corrected. The packages documentation file hash in CanvasPackagesDocsFileHashTest was updated to match the new content.

Upgrade

If your project uses a canvas.config.json that does not explicitly set componentDir, the default has changed from the project root (process.cwd()) to src/components. Add an explicit entry if your components live elsewhere:

{
  "componentDir": "./components"
}

The componentDir path must be inside aliasBaseDir (default src). If your component directory is outside aliasBaseDir, move it or adjust both settings:

{
  "aliasBaseDir": ".",
  "componentDir": "./components"
}

To use static asset or SVG React imports, import them directly from the local codebase and push with the CLI. For TypeScript projects using ?react SVG imports, add the SVGR client types to a Vite environment file:

/// <reference types="vite/client" />
/// <reference types="vite-plugin-svgr/client" />

To opt in to Brand Kit font sync:

npx canvas pull --include-brand-kit
npx canvas push --include-brand-kit

Contribution

Timeline

  • Opened: May 6, 2026
  • Committed: May 12, 2026 (6 days later)

Key contributors

  • balintbrews (Acquia)
  • rikki_iki
  • wotnak (Smartbees)
  • lauriii (Acquia)

This content is AI-generated and may contain errors. See Drupal Digests for more.

item.title: #3581110: Add Multi-Value List Text/Integer Prop Support (UI)
item.description:
• Project: Drupal Canvas
  • Type: Normal feature request
  • Version: 1.x-dev
  • Status: Fixed
  • Changes: 22 files, +1688/−9 · js +1026/−4 · php +367/−5 · css +152 · yml +78 · twig +64 · json +1
  • Diff: 296cea5
  • Discussion: #3581110 · 8 contributors · 29 comments
  • Followers: 10

Summary

The Canvas editor now renders list_string and list_integer fields with a rich multi-select UI: a React Select control showing selected values as removable chips, a searchable dropdown of allowed values, and automatic enforcement of the field's cardinality setting. This feature is active unconditionally for those field types, without requiring the canvas_dev_mode module.

Impact

No upgrade or configuration changes required.

Technical details

On the PHP side, ReduxIntegratedFieldWidgetsHooks previously gated all multi-value UI handling behind a canvas_dev_mode module check. The change adds an OR branch: if the field storage type is list_string or list_integer, the multi-value path runs regardless. Inside that path, markMultivalueFormElements is extended to recognize select elements (in addition to textfield, number, url, etc.) and stamps them with #is_multivalue_form = TRUE, data-field-label, and a new data-cardinality attribute carrying the raw cardinality integer (–1 for unlimited).

canvas_stark_theme_suggestions_select_alter detects #is_multivalue_form and pushes select__multivalue_form as a theme suggestion, causing Canvas Stark to render the select through a new Twig template (select--multivalue-form.html.twig). That template emits a <drupal-canvas-select--multivalue-form> custom element, which the frontend component registry maps to the new DrupalSelectMultivalueForm React component.

DrupalSelectMultivalueForm wraps a hidden native <select multiple> (managed by the existing InputBehaviors HOC for Redux tracking) alongside a react-select multi-select control. When the user picks or removes options, the component updates native select state and fires a synthetic change event so InputBehaviors picks it up. Cardinality enforcement is handled in isOptionDisabled: once the number of local selections reaches the data-cardinality value, any unselected option is disabled; –1 means unlimited.

Two JavaScript fixes accompany the feature. First, InputBehaviorsEntityForm now detects <select multiple> elements and collects all selectedOptions rather than reading .value (which only returns the last-clicked item). Second, both InputBehaviorsEntityForm and PageDataFormRenderer convert array-valued multi-select entries (keyed as field_name[]) into indexed keys (field_name[0], field_name[1], ...) before writing to the Redux store or dispatching setPageData, preventing a PHP Array to string conversion error in ClientDataToEntityConverter::setEntityFields. Empty-array serialization is also fixed in formStateToObject: when the array is empty an empty-string sentinel is appended, and the deserializer normalizes both '' and [''] back to [].

PropShapeRepository test coverage is extended with list_string and list_integer prop shapes (both unlimited and maxItems-bounded), each mapping to options_select widget and canvas_load_allowed_values_for_component_prop storage settings. Cypress entity-form-field-type tests and Playwright multi-value tests cover add, remove, clear-all, cardinality-limit enforcement, and persistence across page reload. This builds on the same multi-value UI pattern introduced for plain text fields in #3573096 and integer/number fields in #3576003.

Contribution

Timeline

  • Opened: March 24, 2026
  • Committed: April 29, 2026 (1 month later)

Key contributors

  • kunal.sachdev (Acquia)
  • utkarsh_33 (Acquia)
  • bnjmnm (Acquia)
  • narendrar (Acquia)
  • tedbow (Acquia)

Collaboration

During review, utkarsh_33 identified two overflow issues: the selected-chips container expanding vertically without bound, and the dropdown menu not being scrollable in both directions. kunal.sachdev addressed the dropdown scrollability in a follow-up commit; the vertical expansion of the chips container was left as a follow-up pending design input. bnjmnm requested that entity-form-field-types-test.cy.js be expanded to cover the new widgets before the patch could be marked RTBC, which was completed before merge.

This content is AI-generated and may contain errors. See Drupal Digests for more.

item.title: #3572553: Save Multi-Value Prop Configuration for Code Components
item.description:
• Project: Drupal Canvas
  • Type: Critical feature request
  • Version: 1.x-dev
  • Status: Fixed
  • Changes: 3 files, +2/−199 · js +2 · php +0/−199
  • Diff: 94e27a4
  • Discussion: #3572553 · 6 contributors · 73 comments
  • Followers: 9

Summary

The Canvas editor can now correctly save and reload multi-value (array) prop configurations for code components. Previously, selected values for array-type props did not persist after adding a component and returning to its page. As part of the fix, a short-lived server-side normalization workaround that had been added to compensate for incorrectly structured client data was identified as technical debt and removed, leaving the backend clean and free of compensating logic.

Impact

No upgrade or configuration changes required.

Technical details

The overall feature work landed in two commits. The first (0920a02a) delivered the core persistence changes. The second, MR !742 (94e27a47), is the corrective diff provided here: it removes JavaScriptComponent::normalizePropsSchema() and the call to it inside updateFromClientSide(). That private method had been moving enum, meta:enum, and x-translation-context keys from the array-schema level down into the items subschema, and also stripping placeholder [[]] examples, to work around clients sending malformed prop data. Wim Leers flagged this as directly opposed to the schema work in #3568264 (which added proper array type support to canvas.json_schema.yml) and as pure technical debt. The correct contract is for the client to submit prop schemas that are already valid, with enum/meta:enum nested under items.

Along with the production code removal, the associated testNormalizePropsSchema test method and its providerNormalizePropsSchema data provider are removed from JavascriptComponentTest, as is the now-unused ConstraintViolationsTestTrait use in that class. The only addition in MR !742 is inline comments in the UI serialization function serializeProps clarifying the hasExample guard logic for array versus non-array props, where string arrays require at least one non-empty value.

Contribution

Timeline

  • Opened: February 10, 2026
  • First commit: March 10, 2026 (28 days later)
  • Last commit: April 28, 2026 (1 month and 3 commits later)

Collaboration

During review, wim leers identified normalizePropsSchema() as technical debt that was "literally opposed to" the schema improvements landed in #3568264, which this issue had been blocked on. After the initial commit included the method, tedbow opened MR !742 specifically to remove it, and wim leers gave LGTM before it was committed.

This content is AI-generated and may contain errors. See Drupal Digests for more.

item.title: #3573776: Canvas needs a way for server to send notifications and trigger actions in the client
item.description:
• Project: Drupal Canvas
  • Type: Normal plan
  • Version: 1.1.0
  • Status: Fixed
  • Discussion: #3573776 · 4 contributors · 15 comments
  • Followers: 9

Summary

Canvas now has a server-to-client notification system that lets Canvas extensions surface messages to authenticated users without a page reload. Notifications appear in two places: an Activity Center dropdown (bell icon in the top bar) and as auto-dismissing toast messages in the top-right corner. Five types are supported: processing, success, info, warning, and error. The entire feature is currently gated behind drupalSettings.canvas.devMode.

Impact

Affects module maintainers and site owners.

  • Database update: Two new tables added: canvas_notifications and canvas_notification_reads.
  • New API: New NotificationService PHP class and GET /canvas/api/v0/notifications / POST /canvas/api/v0/notifications/read REST endpoints for creating and polling notifications from Canvas extensions.

Technical details

The backend stores notifications in two new database tables: canvas_notifications (id, type, key, title, message, timestamp, actions) and canvas_notification_reads (uid, notification_id, timestamp). A NotificationService PHP class handles creation and retrieval. Each notification gets a UUID on creation; an optional key field groups related notifications so that creating a new notification with a given key automatically deletes all prior error, warning, and processing notifications for that key, enabling clean state transitions such as processing -> success or error -> processing on retry.

Two REST endpoints are exposed: GET /canvas/api/v0/notifications returns up to 25 recent non-processing notifications plus all active processing notifications with per-user hasRead state; POST /canvas/api/v0/notifications/read marks a list of notification IDs as read for the current user. A cron hook replaces stale processing notifications (older than 30 minutes) with a timeout error and purges all notifications and read entries older than 30 days.

The frontend uses RTK Query for polling with adaptive frequency: 2 seconds when processing notifications are present, 10 seconds normally, and 5 minutes when the browser tab is not focused. A Redux slice manages UI state. Marking notifications as read uses an optimistic update. The Activity Center is a Radix Popover sorted as: processing at top, then unread errors and warnings (newest first), then everything else chronologically. Toast notifications appear for any notification with a timestamp after page load, auto-dismiss after 15 seconds, and can be dismissed early (which also marks the notification as read).

The design was shaped substantially in the comment thread: justafish proposed the data structure and a full OpenAPI schema including a key-based dismissal mechanism and a separate read endpoint. wim leers pushed back on a dedicated polling endpoint (preferring consolidation with the existing autosave poll) and raised concerns about information disclosure for globally scoped notifications; the final Phase 1 design accepted a dedicated polling endpoint and global-only notifications as acknowledged trade-offs. Notifications in Phase 1 are broadcast to all authenticated users; per-user or per-role scoping is noted as a future enhancement. The implementation was delivered through child issues #3514033, #3550161, and #3574257.

Contribution

Timeline

  • Opened: February 16, 2026

Collaboration

justafish drove the API design by posting a detailed client-side data structure proposal and a complete OpenAPI schema for both the GET and POST endpoints, which became the basis for the final implementation. wim leers raised substantive concerns: whether polling should be consolidated with the existing autosave endpoint, how cross-session read state should be tracked (UUID vs timestamp), and whether global notifications created an information disclosure risk. Those exchanges directly influenced the scope decisions documented in the issue: a dedicated polling endpoint was kept for Phase 1 simplicity, and per-user or per-role scoping was explicitly deferred.

This content is AI-generated and may contain errors. See Drupal Digests for more.

item.title: #3586959: Validate image prop example URLs in Code Component metadata files
item.description:
• Project: Drupal Canvas
  • Type: Normal feature request
  • Version: 1.x-dev
  • Status: Fixed
  • Discussion: #3586959 · 1 contributor · 7 comments
  • Followers: 2

Summary

Canvas Code Component authors now get an ESLint error if their component.yml example values for canvas.module/image props use relative or root-relative src URLs instead of fully-qualified ones. The error message recommends using a placeholder such as https://placehold.co/600x400. This gives faster feedback during local development rather than waiting for backend schema validation to catch the problem. The fix shipped in @drupal-canvas/eslint-config@0.3.0 and @drupal-canvas/cli@0.14.0.

Impact

Affects module maintainers.

  • New API: New ESLint rule component-image-example-urls added at severity error to the required config in @drupal-canvas/eslint-config; enforces fully-qualified URLs for canvas.module/image prop example src values in component.yml files.

Technical details

The issue adds a new ESLint rule component-image-example-urls to the required config in @drupal-canvas/eslint-config. The rule lives in rules/component-image-example-urls.js and operates on the yaml-eslint-parser AST. It only activates for files matched by isComponentYmlFile (i.e. component.yml). For each entry in props.properties, hasImageRef checks whether the prop's mapping contains a $ref equal to json-schema-definitions://canvas.module/image, or whether its items mapping does (covering array-of-image props). When an image prop is found, getInvalidImageExampleNodes walks the examples sequence and inspects each image object's src scalar. isFullyQualifiedUrl uses the standard URL constructor and requires both a non-empty protocol and hostname; anything else (relative paths, root-relative paths) produces an ESLint error with a message pointing to https://placehold.co/600x400. The rule is registered at severity 'error' alongside existing rules such as component-dir-name and component-prop-names. The underlying backend schema gap that permits the same invalid values through the JSON schema validator is tracked separately in #3586958.

Upgrade

Update the ESLint config package directly:

npm install @drupal-canvas/eslint-config@0.3.0

Or, if using the Canvas CLI, upgrade to the new release which pulls in the updated config automatically:

npm install @drupal-canvas/cli@0.14.0

After upgrading, any component.yml file that has an image prop example with a relative or root-relative src (e.g. ./hero.jpg or /sites/default/files/hero.jpg) will produce an ESLint error. Replace those values with fully-qualified URLs such as https://placehold.co/600x400.

Contribution

Timeline

  • Opened: April 26, 2026
  • Committed: April 26, 2026

This content is AI-generated and may contain errors. See Drupal Digests for more.

item.title: #3574994: Resizable right sidebar for Drupal Canvas
item.description:
• Project: Drupal Canvas
  • Type: Normal feature request
  • Version: 1.x-dev
  • Status: Fixed
  • Changes: 15 files, +393/−170 · js +312/−110 · json +42/−14 · css +39/−46
  • Diff: d0a85f2
  • Discussion: #3574994 · 7 contributors · 29 comments
  • Followers: 9

Summary

The right sidebar in Drupal Canvas (where component properties are edited) can now be resized by dragging a handle. Users can drag the handle to adjust the sidebar width between 320px and 640px, and the chosen width is saved in localStorage so it persists across reloads and sessions. The sidebar collapses automatically when no component is selected in the template editor or when interactive mode is active, and expands back to the saved width when a component is selected again.

Impact

No upgrade or configuration changes required.

Technical details

The implementation ships in three commits. The first introduces the allotment library to replace react-resizable-panels throughout the codebase and implements Allotment-based pane splitting in both Editor.tsx and CodeEditorUi.tsx. A new layoutStorage.ts utility (getLayoutItem / setLayoutItem) wraps localStorage with error handling so the UI falls back to defaults when storage is unavailable.

The third commit reworks the main editor sidebar after manual QA revealed that Allotment stores pane sizes as percentages: toggling the left sidebar changes the total container width, which causes the right sidebar to visually shift. The fix introduces a new EditorLayout component (EditorLayout.tsx) that replaces Allotment for the main editor split. EditorLayout uses a custom pointer-event drag implementation: handlePointerDown captures the pointer, pointermove computes the new pixel width by subtracting e.clientX from the layout container's right edge, and pointerup releases capture and persists the final value. A companion editorLayoutStorage.ts stores and loads the width as a plain pixel integer (SIDEBAR_MIN_PX 320px, SIDEBAR_MAX_PX 640px, default 320px). The right column hides with a CSS width: 0; transition: width 0.15s ease-out rather than display: none, so the collapse animates smoothly. AppRoutes now renders EditorLayout instead of Editor directly; Editor is stripped back to its original role of managing context and rendering EditorFrame.

The code editor (CodeEditorUi) continues to use nested Allotment components for its horizontal and vertical splits. Panel sizes are persisted separately under the key canvas-code-editor-layout. The ContextualPanel CSS switches from a fixed width: var(--sidebar-right-width) to flex: 1; min-width: 0 so it fills its EditorLayout-managed column. The --sidebar-right-width CSS variable is removed from the global token set. Playwright and Cypress tests are updated to account for the new visibility transitions: waitForContextualPanel() is called after component selection, and the previewReady() command switches its opacity assertion from .canvasEditorFrameScalingContainer to [data-testid="canvas-editor-frame"].

Contribution

Timeline

  • Opened: February 21, 2026
  • First commit: February 22, 2026 (1 day later)
  • Last commit: April 24, 2026 (2 months and 3 commits later)

Key contributors

  • lauriii (Acquia)
  • balintbrews (Acquia)
  • jessebaker (Acquia)
  • mayur-sose

Collaboration

During manual verification, mayur-sose found that toggling the left sidebar caused the right sidebar width to change (test case 2.0 fail). jessebaker reviewed the report and initially decided it was acceptable behavior, noting that the extra space was simply shared proportionally and fixing it would require significant work for little benefit. lauriii disagreed, pushed back on the issue thread, and submitted a third MR that switched from percentage-based Allotment sizing to a custom pixel-based drag implementation. That approach fully resolved the drift without the complexity jessebaker had anticipated.

item.title: #3586342: Symmetrically translatable component trees, STEP 1: introduce `ComponentInstanceInputsConfigSchemaGeneratorInterface`
item.description:
• Project: Drupal Canvas
  • Type: Major feature request
  • Version: 1.x-dev
  • Status: Fixed
  • Changes: 20 files, +1006/−6 · php +966/−6 · yml +24 · md +16
  • Diff: 4e6093e
  • Discussion: #3586342 · 2 contributors · 9 comments
  • Followers: 4

Summary

This is an internal architectural change with no user-facing effect. It lays the groundwork for symmetrical translation of component tree inputs on multilingual Canvas sites. Once the follow-up issues (#3582478 for config-defined trees and #3583684 for content-defined trees) land, editors will be able to translate individual component inputs in both config entities and content entities using a consistent, per-input translatability model.

Impact

Affects module maintainers.

  • API change: GeneratedFieldExplicitInputUxComponentSourceBase::getExplicitInputDefinitions() changed from protected to public; subclasses that override it as protected will break.
  • New API: New ComponentInstanceInputsConfigSchemaGeneratorInterface with getConfigSchemaMapping() and refineForInstance(), new inputs_config_schema_generator parameter on #[ComponentSource] attribute, new static ComponentInputs::resolveConfigSchemaMapping(), and new ComponentInputs::getTranslatableInputKeys().

Technical details

The core problem is that translating component instance inputs requires knowing which of those inputs are actually translatable, and that knowledge must be consistent between config entity translation (driven by configuration schema discovered by config_translation) and content entity translation (driven by content_translation). Before this change there was no single source of truth for that.

The solution introduces ComponentInstanceInputsConfigSchemaGeneratorInterface with two methods: getConfigSchemaMapping(ComponentSourceInterface $component_source) generates a config schema mapping for all inputs of a component version, and refineForInstance(array $mapping, array $actual_inputs, string $component_id, string $component_version) adjusts that mapping per-instance (for example, removing translatable: true from an input that is populated by a non-static prop source rather than direct user input).

A new inputs_config_schema_generator constructor parameter is added to the ComponentSource attribute, accepting an FQCN implementing this interface. It defaults to FallbackComponentInstanceInputsConfigSchemaGenerator, which emits type: ignore for all inputs and marks nothing as translatable. Three concrete implementations are provided:

  • FallbackComponentInstanceInputsConfigSchemaGenerator: the default, produces type: ignore for every input key with no translatability.
  • BlockComponentInstanceInputsConfigSchemaGenerator: reads the existing block.settings.* config schema via TypedConfigManagerInterface, strips provider, id, and context_mapping, and returns the native block settings mapping. Block settings are plain values so refineForInstance() is a no-op.
  • GeneratedFieldExplicitInputUxComponentInstanceInputsConfigSchemaGenerator: inspects the normalized JSON schema prop shapes from GeneratedFieldExplicitInputUxComponentSourceBase::getExplicitInputDefinitions() and marks a prop as translatable: true when its shape is type: string (with no enum) and its format is absent or one of iri, iri-reference, uri, uri-reference, or when contentMediaType is text/html. In refineForInstance(), any prop whose stored value is not a StaticPropSource has its translatable key removed, because only user-authored static values should be translated.

The static method ComponentInputs::resolveConfigSchemaMapping(string $component_id, string $component_version, array $actual_inputs) is the single point of truth: it loads the Component config entity, resolves the generator class from the plugin definition, instantiates it via ClassResolverInterface (to support container injection as in BlockComponentInstanceInputsConfigSchemaGenerator), and calls getConfigSchemaMapping() then refineForInstance(). A new instance method ComponentInputs::getTranslatableInputKeys() calls resolveConfigSchemaMapping() and filters to keys whose schema definition has translatable: true (using an ephemeral Mapping object to resolve inherited translatability from core's config schema type hierarchy).

BlockComponent and both GeneratedFieldExplicitInputUx-based sources (JsComponent and the SDC source) have their #[ComponentSource] attributes updated to specify their respective generator class.

GeneratedFieldExplicitInputUxComponentSourceBase::getExplicitInputDefinitions() is promoted from protected to public so that GeneratedFieldExplicitInputUxComponentInstanceInputsConfigSchemaGenerator can call it from outside the class hierarchy.

Contribution

Timeline

  • Opened: April 22, 2026
  • Committed: April 23, 2026

Key contributors

  • wim leers (Acquia)
  • tedbow (Acquia)

This content is AI-generated and may contain errors. See Drupal Digests for more.

item.title: #3585978: Include page paths and descriptions in the CLI push/pull commands
item.description:
• Project: Drupal Canvas
  • Type: Normal feature request
  • Version: 1.x-dev
  • Status: Fixed
  • Changes: 14 files, +116/−12 · js +49/−7 · php +48/−4 · yml +10 · json +9/−1
  • Diff: 28245af
  • Discussion: #3585978 · 2 contributors · 7 comments
  • Followers: 3

Summary

The Canvas CLI push and pull commands now sync page path aliases and meta descriptions in addition to title and components. Previously, when pushing a local page spec, the path was always taken from the remote record and description was never transferred at all. After this change, both fields are stored in the local spec file and round-trip correctly through the API.

Impact

Affects module maintainers and site owners.

  • Config change: The authored page spec JSON schema now requires a path field alongside title and elements.
  • New API: The Page interface and ApiService.createPage/updatePage gain a required description: string field; path is now string (not string | null) in those method signatures.

Technical details

The issue builds on #3575874, which first added pages to the CLI commands. Two fields were missing from that initial implementation: path and description.

On the TypeScript CLI side, the Page interface gains a description: string field. pageToAuthoredSpec is refactored to build a shared meta object containing uuid, title, path, and description, which is spread into both the empty-components and populated-components return values. preparePages reads description and path from the local JSON spec (both optional, defaulting to ''). pushPages now passes description and page.path (from the spec file) when calling ApiService.createPage and ApiService.updatePage, replacing the previous behaviour of using remotePage.path on update and a hard-coded empty string on create. The path parameter type in both API service methods also tightens from string | null to string. The JSON schema (page-spec.schema.json) adds path to the required array and adds an optional description string property. parsePageSpec adds both path and description to its allowedTopLevelKeys set.

On the Drupal PHP side, ApiContentControllers adds description to the field loop that checks edit access during PATCH handling and passes it through on POST entity creation. A new private method normalizeWithMetadataAndComponents wraps the existing normalizeWithComponents, appending the description field value from the entity (guarded by a hasField check and a view access check) and moving links to the end of the array. All three controller actions (GET single, PATCH, POST/duplicate) switch from normalizeWithComponents to normalizeWithMetadataAndComponents. The OpenAPI spec adds description (type string, "The meta description.") to the page response schema and to the example payloads.

Contribution

Timeline

  • Opened: April 21, 2026
  • Committed: April 21, 2026

Key contributors

item.title: #3585854: Update JS packages
item.description:
• Project: Drupal Canvas
  • Type: Normal task
  • Version: 1.x-dev
  • Status: Closed (fixed)
  • Changes: 4 files, +130/−160 · json +130/−160
  • Diff: 2d3e7e4
  • Discussion: #3585854 · 1 contributor · 10 comments
  • Followers: 3

Summary

The Canvas module's JavaScript dependency tree had accumulated 20 npm audit vulnerabilities (1 low, 12 moderate, 6 high, 1 critical). This update bumps all affected packages so that npm audit reports zero vulnerabilities. There are no user-interface changes; the impact is entirely on the build toolchain used by developers working with Canvas.

Impact

Affects site owners.

  • Security: 20 npm audit vulnerabilities (1 low, 12 moderate, 6 high, 1 critical) resolved to zero by bumping axios, follow-redirects, dompurify, lodash, vite, and related transitive dependencies.

Technical details

The commit updates package.json and package-lock.json across the monorepo. The most security-relevant changes are: axios bumped from 1.13.6 to 1.15.1 (with a companion bump of proxy-from-env from 1.x to 2.x), follow-redirects from 1.15.11 to 1.16.0, dompurify from 3.3.2 to 3.4.0 (also drops the node >= 20 engine constraint), and lodash/lodash-es from 4.17.23 to 4.18.1. Additional routine bumps include vite 6.4.1 → 6.4.2 and 7.3.1 → 7.3.2, astro 5.18.0 → 5.18.1, brace-expansion 1.1.12 → 1.1.14 across several nested dependents, hono 4.12.8 → 4.12.14, protobufjs 7.5.4 → 7.5.5, and yaml pinned to ^2.8.3 via a root overrides entry (removing several stale per-package yaml@1.10.3 overrides that are no longer needed). The axios version constraint in the two package.json files under packages/ is updated from ^1.9.0 / ^1.7.8 to ^1.15.1.

Upgrade

No code changes are required for sites using Canvas. Developers maintaining a fork or working in the Canvas monorepo should run npm install after pulling this commit to sync their local node_modules to the updated lockfile.

Contribution

Timeline

  • Opened: April 20, 2026

item.title: #3585314: Add logo.url and favicon.url to site data for code components
item.description:
• Project: Drupal Canvas
  • Type: Normal feature request
  • Version: 1.x-dev
  • Status: Fixed
  • Changes: 10 files, +181/−4 · php +110/−1 · yml +57/−3 · js +14
  • Diff: 648bb9d
  • Discussion: #3585314 · 2 contributors · 9 comments
  • Followers: 4

Summary

The Canvas module's getSiteData() function now exposes the active theme's logo and favicon through a new themeAssets field. Code components can access getSiteData().themeAssets.logo.url and getSiteData().themeAssets.favicon.url (plus favicon.mimeType) without reaching outside Canvas's data layer. This is a data-layer addition with no admin UI changes.

Impact

Affects module maintainers.

  • New API: New themeAssets field on the SiteData type returned by getSiteData(), exposing logo.url, favicon.url, and favicon.mimeType from the active theme; backed by the new canvas/canvasData.v0.themeAssets Drupal library and CodeComponentDataProvider::getCanvasDataThemeAssetsV0().

Technical details

Canvas exposes Drupal data to JavaScript code components through the drupalSettings.canvasData.v0.* namespace, with each data slice backed by a dedicated Drupal asset library and a PHP provider method. Before this change, getSiteData() covered branding (site name, slogan, home URL) and baseUrl but had no canonical way to supply theme logo or favicon paths.

The fix adds a new slice following the existing pattern:

PHP (CodeComponentDataProvider): getCanvasDataThemeAssetsV0() reads theme_get_setting('logo.url'), theme_get_setting('favicon.url'), and theme_get_setting('favicon.mimetype'), returning them under the v0.themeAssets key.

PHP (ComponentSourceHooks): 'canvas/canvasData.v0.themeAssets' => 'getCanvasDataThemeAssetsV0' is added to ASSET_LIBRARY_METHOD_MAPPING. The jsSettingsAlter() conditional block populates the setting only when themeAssets.logo.url is still NULL, preserving any value set earlier and allowing downstream overrides via array_replace_recursive.

Asset library (canvas.libraries.yml): A new canvasData.v0.themeAssets library entry is declared with drupalSettings defaults (logo.url: null, favicon.url: null, favicon.mimeType: null). The parent canvasData.v0 library's dependency list and the canvas.js_component.* config schema Choice constraint are both updated to include v0.themeAssets (with the full list alphabetically ordered, which also corrects the previous ordering of v0.jsonapiSettings).

TypeScript (drupal-utils.ts): A ThemeAssets interface is defined with logo: { url: string } and favicon: { url: string; mimeType: string }. SiteData gains a themeAssets: ThemeAssets field, and getSiteData() reads window.drupalSettings?.canvasData?.v0?.themeAssets with a safe fallback. The DrupalSettings type definition is also extended to match.

  • Committed: April 17, 2026

item.title: #3582921: Support OAuth 2.0 authorization_code login flow for Canvas CLI
item.description:
• Project: Drupal Canvas
  • Type: Normal feature request
  • Version: 1.x-dev
  • Status: Fixed
  • Changes: 18 files, +1447/−52 · js +1343/−47 · md +102/−5 · json +1 · txt +1
  • Diff: d1fd817
  • Discussion: #3582921 · 2 contributors · 8 comments
  • Followers: 4

Summary

The Canvas CLI gains an interactive browser-based login command, canvas login, so site administrators and developers can authenticate as their own Drupal user instead of using a shared service-account secret. Running npx canvas login --site-url https://example.com opens a browser, prompts the user to log in to Drupal, and stores the resulting tokens locally. Subsequent canvas push and canvas pull commands use those stored tokens automatically, with no CANVAS_CLIENT_ID or CANVAS_CLIENT_SECRET environment variables required. A matching canvas logout command removes the stored credentials. To use this flow, a Consumer in Drupal admin must have the Authorization Code grant type enabled, PKCE checked, and http://localhost:4444/callback registered as a redirect URI.

Impact

No upgrade or configuration changes required.

Technical details

The implementation adds three new files to the packages/cli workspace. src/services/auth.ts handles the full OAuth 2.0 flow: discoverAuth() fetches /.well-known/oauth-protected-resource with an X-Canvas-CLI: 1 header to obtain the authorization server base URL and reads the X-Consumer-ID response header to auto-detect the client ID; if that endpoint returns 404 it falls back to Simple OAuth's conventional /oauth/authorize and /oauth/token paths on the site. It then fetches /.well-known/openid-configuration from the auth server to resolve the final authorization_endpoint and token_endpoint. PKCE helpers (generateCodeVerifier, deriveCodeChallenge via SHA-256, generateState) produce the code verifier and S256 challenge. waitForCallback(port) starts a temporary http.createServer listener on http://localhost:PORT/callback with a 5-minute timeout, validates the returned state parameter to guard against CSRF, and resolves with the authorization code. exchangeCode() posts the authorization_code grant with the code verifier to the token endpoint. src/lib/token-store.ts persists the access token, optional refresh token, expiry timestamp, client ID, and token endpoint to ~/.config/drupal-canvas/oauth.json (written as a JSON file with mode 0o600, keyed by normalized site URL) using getTokenEntry, setTokenEntry, and removeTokenEntry. src/commands/login.ts wires these together into loginCommand and logoutCommand registered with Commander. The commit diff also updates the module README to document the Consumer configuration steps (enable Authorization Code grant, check Use PKCE, uncheck Is Confidential, register the callback URI) and adds a note that scopes used with canvas login must have the Authorization Code grant type enabled.

Upgrade

To use canvas login, configure a Consumer in Drupal admin at /admin/config/services/consumer:

  1. Enable the Authorization Code grant type.
  2. Check Use PKCE? and leave Is Confidential? unchecked.
  3. Add http://localhost:4444/callback as a Redirect URI (use a different port if you pass --port).
  4. Enable the Authorization Code grant type on each scope the CLI will request.

Then run:

npx canvas login --site-url https://example.com --client-id YOUR-CLIENT-ID

Tokens are stored in ~/.config/drupal-canvas/oauth.json. Existing client_credentials setups are unaffected.

Contribution

Timeline

  • Opened: April 2, 2026
  • Committed: April 16, 2026 (13 days later)

item.title: #3578452: Include pages with images in the CLI push/pull commands
item.description:
• Project: Drupal Canvas
  • Type: Normal feature request
  • Version: 1.x-dev
  • Status: Fixed
  • Changes: 28 files, +1461/−42 · js +1306/−42 · php +88 · md +38 · yml +24 · json +5
  • Diff: 24bda41
  • Discussion: #3578452 · 3 contributors · 8 comments
  • Followers: 4

Summary

The Canvas CLI can now handle pages that contain image props during both push and pull. On pull, the resolved image data (src, alt, width, height) is written to the local JSON spec alongside a new _provenance field that preserves the Drupal media entity reference. A new npx canvas reconcile-media command downloads external images referenced in local specs, uploads them to Drupal, and records the resulting media entity IDs in _provenance. The push command now guards against unreconciled external image URLs and directs users to run reconcile-media first. A new canvas:media:view OAuth scope is also installed automatically on existing sites via a database update.

Impact

Affects module maintainers and site owners.

  • Database update: Post-update hook canvas_oauth_post_update_0004_media_view_scope creates the canvas:media:view OAuth scope on existing sites.
  • Config change: New canvas_media_view OAuth scope config entity installed by canvas_oauth_post_update_0004_media_view_scope.
  • New API: New reconcile-media CLI command and ApiService.uploadMedia() method for uploading external images to Drupal media entities.

Technical details

The core challenge: json-render page specs store resolved media props (src/alt/width/height) for local rendering, but Drupal's API expects entity references (target_id/target_uuid). The solution is a new _provenance field on AuthoredSpecElement that holds the Drupal-native form of media inputs alongside the human-readable props.

Pull direction (pageToAuthoredSpec): A new extractMediaProvenance helper compares each component node's inputs and inputs_resolved. When a prop in inputs_resolved looks like resolved media (has a src string) and the corresponding entry in inputs is an entity reference (target_id or target_uuid), that inputs value is written as _provenance[propName] in the local spec file.

Push direction (serializeElementMapForServer): A new mediaTransformer is first in the transformer chain in prop-transforms.ts. When _provenance carries a target_id or target_uuid for a media prop, only that key is forwarded to Drupal (the CLI-only source_url is stripped). serializePropsForServer receives the element's _provenance map as a third argument so each prop transformer can access it via the context object.

reconcile-media command (commands/reconcile-media.ts):

  1. Scans all local page specs with collectUnreconciledMediaProps, which calls getUnreconciledMedia per prop. A prop is unreconciled when its src is an absolute external URL or a data: URL.
  2. Deduplicates by URL so each image is downloaded and uploaded only once.
  3. Downloads via downloadExternalMedia (axios with request-filtering-agent) or downloadDataUrlMedia for inline data URLs.
  4. Validates the MIME type against SUPPORTED_IMAGE_TYPES (jpeg, png, gif, webp, avif); SVG is explicitly rejected.
  5. Uploads via the new ApiService.uploadMedia() method, which POSTs multipart/form-data to /canvas/api/v0/media/:mediaType/upload (the endpoint added in #3578449).
  6. Applies results: updates props[propName] to inputs_resolved from Drupal and writes _provenance[propName] = { target_id, source_url }.
  7. Writes updated specs back to disk.

Push guard: preparePages now calls collectUnreconciledMediaProps per page and aggregates the results as pendingMediaReconciliations. If any are found, pushCommand throws before contacting Drupal, instructing the user to run npx canvas reconcile-media.

OAuth scope: A new canvas_media_view config entity and canvas_oauth_post_update_0004_media_view_scope post-update hook ensure the canvas:media:view scope exists on upgraded sites. The CLI's default scope string (DEFAULT_SCOPE) now includes canvas:media:image:create and canvas:media:view.

handleApiError fix: The method's return type was corrected from void to never, which allowed removal of all unreachable throw statements that followed calls to it throughout ApiService.

This work builds on the json-render conversion utilities from #3573996 and the inputs_resolved computed property from #3577654.

Upgrade

Run database updates to install the new canvas:media:view OAuth scope on existing sites:

drush updb

For CLI users: if your local page specs contain image props whose src is an external URL, run the new reconcile step before pushing:

npx canvas reconcile-media
npx canvas push

Contribution

  • Opened: March 11, 2026
  • Committed: April 16, 2026 (1 month later)

item.title: #3585055: Add preview-build command to Workbench
item.description:
• Project: Drupal Canvas
  • Type: Normal feature request
  • Version: 1.x-dev
  • Status: Closed (fixed)
  • Changes: 13 files, +2886/−39 · js +2766/−37 · mdx +69 · md +48 · json +2/−2 · txt +1
  • Diff: 53ad1c0
  • Discussion: #3585055 · 1 contributor · 6 comments
  • Followers: 4

Summary

The canvas-workbench CLI gains a new preview-build command in @drupal-canvas/workbench@0.3.0. Developers can now export a self-contained HTML preview for a single component or page without running the full Workbench dev server. The output is portable: each HTML file bundles fonts, images, and other local assets inline as base64 data URLs, so the artifact can be moved, shared, or used in review pipelines and downstream tooling without any additional serving infrastructure.

Impact

Affects module maintainers.

  • New API: New preview-build subcommand and exported functions buildPreviewArtifact, parsePreviewBuildArgs, runPreviewBuildCli in @drupal-canvas/workbench@0.3.0.

Technical details

The CLI entry point (bin.mjs) gains a new branch that detects the preview-build subcommand and routes it to a dedicated script (dist/server/src/server/preview-build.mjs) via a refactored runChild() helper, leaving the existing Vite-backed dev / build path unchanged.

The new preview-build.mjs module exports parsePreviewBuildArgs, buildPreviewArtifact, and runPreviewBuildCli. parsePreviewBuildArgs validates the --component-path / --page-path (exactly one required), --out-dir (required), and optional --pretty flags and returns a typed result object. buildPreviewArtifact accepts injectable dependencies (buildPreviewPayload, discover, resolveConfig, extractMetadata, bundleInteractivePreview) so it can be tested without filesystem side-effects. For component targets it writes component-default.html plus zero-padded numbered mock files (component-mock-01.html, etc.); for page targets it writes page-default.html. A manifest.json with portable relative path values and targetType, target.hasMocks, target.mockCount, and per-entry labels accompanies every export.

Writes are atomic: all HTML files are prepared in a sibling staging directory first, then the directory is renamed into place. If an error occurs the staging directory is removed and any pre-existing output directory is restored from a backup rename, so a failed build never corrupts previously exported artifacts.

Path constraints are enforced against the configured componentDir and pagesDir from canvas.config.json. Paths outside those roots are rejected with an error message that names the configured directory and explains that preview discovery, mocks, and @/ module resolution are scoped to those roots.

The companion preview-payload module (preview-payload.mjs) houses buildPreviewPayload and bundleInteractivePreview, which use Vite in library mode with assetsInlineLimit: Infinity to inline all local assets. The React client entry (main.tsx) gains ensurePreviewRuntimeGlobals(), which initializes window.React and window.drupalSettings.canvasData.v0.baseUrl as defaults so exported HTML files work when opened without the Workbench server's runtime scaffold. The command always writes one JSON payload to stdout covering request, target, renderMode (interactive), outDir, manifestPath, summary counts, warnings, and errors.

Contribution

Timeline

  • Opened: April 15, 2026
  • Committed: April 16, 2026

Key contributors

  • wotnak (Smartbees)

item.title: #3583916: Create a new command for adding symlinks for agentic skills after repository has been scaffolded
item.description:
• Project: Drupal Canvas
  • Type: Normal feature request
  • Version: 1.x-dev

Summary

The @drupal-canvas/create CLI now includes an agents subcommand that lets developers set up AI agent compatibility symlinks in an existing project, not just during initial scaffolding. Previously, if agent setup was skipped at scaffold time or a developer wanted to add support for additional agents later, there was no way to do it without rescaffolding. Running npx @drupal-canvas/create@latest agents (optionally with a comma-separated agent list or none) fills that gap.

Impact

Affects module maintainers.

  • New API: New agents subcommand in @drupal-canvas/create: npx @drupal-canvas/create@latest agents [agents]

Technical details

The core problem was that setupAgentSkills and the interactive agent-selection prompt were only reachable through the main create flow. The fix introduces a standalone agentsCommand function in a new src/agents.ts file, registered on the commander program instance in index.ts via agentsCommand(program). It accepts an optional [agents] argument (comma-separated list or "none"), resolves the project directory from process.cwd(), and delegates to the existing setupAgentSkills and parseAgentSelection helpers. To support context-appropriate error messages when called from the new command, parseAgentSelection gains an optional label parameter (defaulting to '--agents') that replaces the previously hard-coded string in all three error paths. Two smaller UX improvements landed alongside: setupAgentSkills now logs an informational message when no .agents/skills directory is found (previously it silently returned), and defaultPromptForAgents emits an empty p.log.message('') to add visual spacing before the multiselect prompt.
